scenario: Cardinal Bank reviews 9000 loans per day on a Camunda 8.8 platform and a modeler is renaming the existing 'RiskScore' decision table to 'Customer Risk Score' so that Operate and Tasklist show the new label. The reviewer wants to confirm where the decision name lives in DMN XML and how it is changed through the Camunda Modeler UI.
question: Where is the decision name stored in DMN XML and how is it edited from the Camunda Modeler UI?

options.text: The decision name lives in the name attribute on the decisionTable element and is edited via the properties panel after selecting the table header cell
The decision name lives in the name attribute on the definitions element and is edited via the properties panel of the DRD root container node
The decision name lives in the label attribute on the decision element and is edited inline by renaming the decision node in the DRD canvas
The decision name lives in the name attribute on the decision element and is edited via the properties panel after selecting the decision in the DRD view

a.text: Incorrect. The decisionTable element holds the table logic, not the user-facing decision name; the name attribute that describes the decision lives one level up on the decision element.

b.text: Incorrect. The name attribute on definitions labels the surrounding DRG, not an individual decision; renaming a decision through that attribute would rename the whole graph.

c.text: Incorrect. The DMN decision element exposes its label through the standard name attribute, not a label attribute, so editing a non-existent attribute would not update the name.

d.text: Correct. The docs state that the decision name is the name attribute on the decision element and can be changed via the properties panel after selecting the Decision in the DRD view.

explanation: The user-facing decision name is the name attribute on the decision element and is the value the modeler displays for that node. From the UI the modeler selects the decision in the Decision Requirements Diagram view and edits the name through the properties panel on the right.
